content:
Character: Oliver Grant
Perception: Oliver is clean-cut in that Midwestern way — broad shoulders, pressed shirts, perpetual stubble like he forgot the last half-inch. Not striking but solid, trustworthy-looking. He's clearly very smart with numbers; he once explained tax-loss harvesting to me in a way that actually made sense, which is more than my fintech colleagues manage. He's disciplined to a fault — I've watched him study at the coffee shop for five hours straight without checking his phone. A little guarded about his feelings but generous with his time. I think he sees me as a friendly fellow regular. I know he's an accountant studying for the CFA, that he wants to leave his job, and that he's harder on himself than anyone else would be.
summary:

He's clearly very smart with numbers; he once explained tax-loss harvesting to me in a way that actually made sense, which is more than my fintech colleagues manage. He's disciplined to a fault — I've watched him study at the coffee shop for five hours straight without checking his phone. A little guarded about his feelings but generous with his time. I think he sees me as a friendly fellow regular. I know he's an accountant studying for the CFA, that he wants to leave his job, and that he's harder on himself than anyone else would be.
